summary: Phase 3B HR Admin Settings UI backend testing: 19/19 new endpoint tests passed + 23/23 Phase 3A regression passed (42/42 total). All new /api/hr endpoints work correctly — settings GET/PATCH with audit logging, holidays GET/import-indian/copy-from, leave-types CRUD with system-type protection, approver config GET/PATCH, simulator, eligible approvers, and audit-log. Permission isolation verified (sales_executive correctly receives 403 on admin endpoints). The /hr-admin → /hr prefix rename is fully functional. Phase 3A flow (punch in/out, leave apply, sandwich detection, monthly cap, 2-stage approval) is unchanged.

minor.issue: Phase 3A test_apply_sandwich_* failed initially due to stale 'approved' leave (id 56d04c0c, 2026-07-17 to 2026-07-20) from a previous Phase 3A run — not a Phase 3B regression. After deleting the stale record from leave_requests, both tests passed. Phase 3A test file is not idempotent because once a leave gets 'approved' through the 2-stage flow, the next run overlaps. Not a bug in code, but a test-data hygiene gap.

action_items: (Optional) Make test_phase3a_attendance_leaves.py idempotent — clean up created leave_requests at the start/end of the sandwich tests so it can re-run without manual DB cleanup. Currently 56d04c0c-style 'approved' leftovers cause overlap errors on re-run.
No code fixes required for Phase 3B — all 19 endpoints functional and permission-gated correctly.
critical_code_review_comments: hr_admin.py docstring still references the old '/hr-admin/...' prefix at top of file even though router now uses '/hr'. Cosmetic only — update doc comment for clarity.

hr_admin.py is 621 lines — still under 700-line threshold but approaching the limit. Consider splitting into hr_settings.py / hr_holidays.py / hr_leave_types.py / hr_approvers.py when adding more endpoints.

policy_audit_log collection is lazily created and silently swallows any insert exception (try/except pass). Audit failures are invisible — consider logging the exception so silent audit drops can be diagnosed later.

import_indian_holidays endpoint comments say 'standard 11 India national holidays' but DEFAULT_HOLIDAYS_2026 actually contains 9 entries (verified by /api/hr/holidays?year=2026 returning 9). Update docstring or seed data so they match.
copy_holidays_from_year doesn't write an audit entry (other bulk ops like import-indian do). Add _log_audit call for parity.

PATCH /api/hr/settings updates with `v is not None` filter — this prevents clearing fields back to null/false. For example, you cannot set enforce_sandwich_leave back to False after setting it True. Use exclude_unset alone or a sentinel.
test_credentials: Admin: admin@leamss.com / Admin@123 | Sales Exec: sexec-test@leamss.com / Pass@1234 | Sales Mgr: smgr-test@leamss.com / Pass@1234
seed_data_creation: Phase 3B tests created holidays for 2027 (via import-indian) and 2028 (via copy-from 2026). Per request, these were left intact as valid test data. Tests also create+update+delete a custom 'study_leave' leave type during run (cleaned up by test_delete_custom_leave_type). Stale leave_requests record id 56d04c0c (2026-07-17 to 2026-07-20, status=approved) was manually deleted from MongoDB to unblock Phase 3A sandwich regression tests.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 3B endpoints all live under /api/hr (NOT /api/hr-admin). 19 dedicated tests in /app/backend/tests/test_phase3b_hr_admin.py cover every endpoint specified by the review. Phase 3A test file /app/backend/tests/test_phase3a_attendance_leaves.py is not fully idempotent — if sandwich tests fail with 'Overlapping leave request exists', delete the approved leave_requests document with from_date=2026-07-17 for user 44bd0424-9e5b-4390-8dd2-ec0ecb50253f. Holidays for 2027 & 2028 are seeded in DB (left intentionally). Custom leave types are auto-cleaned by the test suite.
rca of the issue: Initial Phase 3A regression showed 2 failures (test_apply_sandwich_requires_ack, test_apply_sandwich_accepted). Reproduction: both tests apply leave for sexec-test from 2026-07-17 to 2026-07-20. Root cause: prior Phase 3A run completed the full 2-stage approval workflow leaving a leave_request with status='approved' (id 56d04c0c). Backend correctly returns 400 'Overlapping leave request exists' because it's the right business rule. Mitigation: deleted that one stale leave_request via DB connection in /app/backend/core/database. Long-term fix is to add teardown to those two tests that removes the leave they created.
